stem: The function f is defined by f(x) = (x − 1)(x + 1)(x − 3). Which of the following describes the graph of y = f(x) in the xy-plane?

answerOptions.content: A graph that crosses the x-axis only at (0, 0).
A graph that crosses the x-axis at (−1, 0), (1, 0), and (3, 0) only, with no y-intercept.
A graph that crosses the x-axis at (−1, 0), (1, 0), and (3, 0), and the y-axis at (0, 3).
A graph that crosses the x-axis at (1, 0) and (3, 0) only.

rationale:
Choice C is correct. Setting f(x) = 0 gives x = −1, 1, or 3, so the graph crosses the x-axis at (−1, 0), (1, 0), and (3, 0). Substituting x = 0 gives f(0) = (−1)(1)(−3) = 3, so the y-intercept is (0, 3).

Choices A, B, and D are inconsistent with one or more of these features of the graph.
